decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader width to enhance lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries. This is a conservative geometric change within bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader width maximizes the lateral area available for heat conduction towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries, reducing thermal resistance.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the spreader layer
risk_notes: Approaching the design domain width limit (1.2m); future width increases may be constrained by geometry bounds rather than performance.
Diminishing returns as the spreader approaches the full width of the cooled boundaries.
